title: phanera cardinalis
intro: Phanera cardinalis, also known as the Cardinal Climber, is a type of climbing plant species native to tropical Asia, belonging to the family Moraceae. Traditionally, Phanera cardinalis has been used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Phanera cardinalis include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and support cardiovascular health, as well as its use in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. Phanera cardinalis can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or decoctions, and is also used in traditional medicine to make poultices and ointments for topical application.
